g.data-narration: Welcome to learning about data item granularity and Multiple Granularity Locking in database systems.
g.text: Granularity of Data Items and Multiple Granularity Locking

g.data-narration: Granularity determines the size of data items that can be locked, affecting concurrency and system overhead in databases.
g.text: size of data items that can

g.data-narration: The database hierarchy has five levels: database, file, page, record, and field - each with different lock scopes.
Fine-grained locks maximize concurrency but increase overhead and deadlock risk, while coarse-grained locks do the opposite.
Multiple Granularity Locking allows locks at multiple hierarchy levels simultaneously, balancing concurrency with overhead efficiently.
g.text: MGL solves the granularity trade-off by allowing locks at multiple levels
of the database hierarchy simultaneously using intent locks.

g.data-narration: Shared (S) locks allow reading data, while Exclusive (X) locks are needed for both reading and modifying data items.
Intent locks (IS, IX, SIX) indicate the intention to acquire locks at lower hierarchy levels before doing so.
g.text: S on current + IX on children

g.data-narration: The compatibility matrix shows which lock types can coexist. IS and IX are compatible with each other but not with X.
MGL requires acquiring locks top-down from root to leaf, and releasing locks bottom-up from leaf to root.
g.text: To acquire S lock at level i:
Must hold IS or S at level i-1
To acquire X lock at level i:
Must hold IX, SIX, or X at level i-1

g.data-narration: Example: Transaction T1 reading Record1 requires IS locks on Database, File, Page, then S lock on Record1.
Example: T2 updating R1 and R2 needs IX locks on parent nodes and X locks on the target records.
g.text: Example 2: T2 updates R1, R2 on Page1
Lock path: IX on Database → File1 → Page1, then X on R1 and R2

g.data-narration: SIX lock combines S and IX - useful when reading an entire file while updating specific records within it.
g.text: T3 reads entire Employee table and updates one specific record
